fact: July 28 is exactly 209 days into the year, which means there are only 156 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat time - the sun has been baking the ground for weeks, making sidewalks hot enough to fry an egg!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around this date because the heat makes them super active - they can buzz at 120 decibels, louder than a chainsaw!
Fireflies are typically at their most magical around July 28, flashing their brightest light shows during peak mating season!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the dead of winter - penguins are huddling together while we're sweating!
The sun rises super early around July 28 in northern places like Alaska - some spots get sunrise at 4:30 AM!
Corn plants often have growth spurts right now - they can actually grow up to 4 inches in a single day during this hot period!
This date falls in the middle of Leo season in the zodiac, when the lion constellation is prominent in the night sky!
Ocean water typically reaches some of its warmest temperatures around this time in many places, making it perfect for swimming!
Bats are usually having feeding frenzies because July 28 brings peak insect populations - a single bat can gobble up 1,000 mosquitoes in one hour!
The Delta Aquariids meteor shower often peaks right around this date, shooting up to 20 'falling stars' per hour across the night sky!
Tree sap gets super sticky and gooey during this hot time of year - it's like nature's glue gun went crazy!
